query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "grepseek: training search agents for direct corpus interaction" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (d) as it provides foundational methodology and empirical evidence for the core mechanism of "training search agents for direct corpus interaction" (specifically using tree search for multi-step planning in interactive environments), which is the central technical component of the user's proposed "GrepSeek" extension. Although the user mentions "linguistics," the primary research gap in extending a search-agent framework is the algorithmic mechanism itself,
